Isidora may have been a mistranslation

Isidora is a fictional city described in Italo Calvino’s 1972 novel *Invisible Cities*
Isidora is a city filled with the physical embodiment of the perfect desires of youth.  Desires of learning, culture, beauty, sex, impromptu violence…anything that might swirl around in a young traveler’s heart and mind. 
Wanda’s city is supposed to be based on this if I still remember it right

back then when Wanda first debuted I have a discussion with some people here about Wanda references 
- The name Wanda is from the Wanda in Venus in Furs, which is referenced in her E3 name
- The city is based on the The Isidora City from Italo Calvino’s novel
- Her profile also has bible elements
so our conclusion back then is that the city name is Isidora
it was before we got the eng translation
if the CN name is indeed Isarawu then I’m sorry <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>

Sure! In the story, it's called 荒星 "desolate"/"wild" “star”/"planet"; its actual name is lost in time. "desolate planet" is a generic universal way of referring to all these forgotten, dead stars.
currently the term “荒星” is not unified across multiple texts, we're fixing it once we get all the character background stories translated properly
